{{ block title }} Part Four {{ endblock }} {{ block content }}

Finally, for Part Four we will select three different participants in the room for payment (one for each of three separate choice problems). If you are selected for payment in Part Four, we will randomly select one of the decisions within your choice problem, and pay you for that decision based on your selection.

In previous sessions for this experiment there were two distinct rules for the Buyers' choices in Part One.

  • The Single Offer session was identical to this experimental session, where the Seller chose a fixed amount of Widgets to offer to the Buyer. The Buyer then made a choice between the offered amount of Widgets, or to purchase zero Widgets.
  • The Menu Offer session was different from this experimental session, where the Seller instead chose a menu of Widgets to the Buyer (a minimum and maximum number of Widgets on offer). The Buyer then made a choice between any amount of Widgets on the menu, or to purchase zero Widgets.
For both sessions the Buyer's ideal quantity was a fair draw from either the Low, Middle or High urn, just as in your session, where the Seller did not know what value had been drawn.

In each of Part 4 decisions, we will ask you to make your best guess on the difference in average round-payments to the Buyers under the different rules. To incentivize you in thinking through this difference each choice problem will offer you the choice between the actual average payment in the relevant sessions (plus a potential bonus payment). Because of this, you should think carefully about how you think the change in rules affects the Buyer's outcome under each of the three urns.

On the next page, you will be able to explore the interface that the Seller and Buyers used in the Menu Offer session, to help you form your guess on how this affected the Buyer's average payment.

{{ next_button }} {{ endblock }}
